title: HUYA sets $50M share repurchase plan
content:
* HUYA (HUYA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HUYA]) board has authorized a new share repurchase program, effective immediately on March 18, 2026.
 	* Under the program, the company may repurchase up to US$50M of its American depositary shares and/or ordinary shares over a 24-month period ending on March 18, 2028. It expects to fund repurchases made under the program using its existing funds. 
 	* The company's previous share repurchase program, which was originally adopted on August 15, 2023, was scheduled to expire on March 31, 2026.
 	* Upon the effectiveness of the 2026 program, the 2023 Share Repurchase Program was terminated, and any of the unutilized quota of US$24.5M was cancelled.
 	* HUYA +1.26% premarket to $3.19
 	* Source: Press Release [https://seekingalpha.com/pr/20441972-huya-inc-announces-new-us-50-million-share-repurchase-program]

title: Huya outlines expanded game publishing, targets new monetization with content-driven strategy and AI integration
content:
Earnings Call Insights: HUYA Inc. (HUYA) Q4 2025

MANAGEMENT VIEW

 	* Acting Co-CEO Junhong Huang reported "2025 was a record year for us. In the fourth quarter, total net revenues grew by 16% to RMB 1.74 billion, the highest in the last 10 quarters, while our live streaming business remained stable." He highlighted a 59% year-over-year growth for game-related services, advertising, and other businesses, emphasizing a successful transformation into gaming. 
 	* Huang stated the company recorded a one-off accounting provision and, excluding that, would have seen "3 consecutive quarters of positive growing operating profit and the full year operating profit." The user base was described as stable, with "Huya's total MAUs were approximately 160 million" in Q4.
 	* He announced the launch success of Goose Goose Duck Mobile, noting it "attracted over 5 million new users within the first 24 hours and surpassing 10 million within 6 days... ranked #1 on the iOS free game chart for most of the time since its launch." Monetization for this title remains limited for now, but major updates are planned later this year.
 	* Huang also noted the expansion of in-game item sales, including securing "exclusive presale rights for the MVP scheme in Honor of Kings," and partnerships with top developers like Tencent and NetEase. AI integration in live streaming and game tools was highlighted, as was international expansion and a focus on content-driven marketing.
 	* "In the fourth quarter, we delivered accelerated top line growth driven by robust expansion in our game-related services and advertising businesses. However, our operating results were impacted by a onetime RMB 66 million provision, which led to a non-GAAP operating loss of RMB 36 million for the quarter. Excluding the impact of this onetime item, we continue to see improvement in our core operating performance and the overall earnings profile," said CFO Lei Peng.

OUTLOOK

 	* Management indicated a strong publishing pipeline with "multiple new titles coming later this year" and expects monetization of Goose Goose Duck Mobile to increase with upcoming content updates.
 	* Huang stated, "Looking ahead, we will continue to expand customized rights offerings, joint marketing and localized partnerships across more top-tier titles to further enhance user satisfaction."
 	* Peng forecasted, "As game publishing and in-game item sales business continue to grow and as operating leverage gradually improve, we expect to see continued gross margin improvement."
 	* No explicit updates to forward guidance figures or analyst estimate comparisons were mentioned in the call.

FINANCIAL RESULTS

 	* Total net revenues for Q4 were RMB 1.74 billion, up 16% year-over-year. Game-related services, advertising, and other revenues reached RMB 593 million, up 59% from the prior year. Live streaming revenues were RMB 1.15 billion, up 2%.
 	* Cost of revenues grew by 30% year-over-year to RMB 1.49 billion. Gross profit was RMB 245 million for Q4, with a gross margin of 14.1%, up from 11.4% a year ago. Non-GAAP gross profit was RMB 248 million, non-GAAP gross margin 14.3%.
 	* Operating loss narrowed to RMB 65 million. Non-GAAP operating loss, excluding share-based compensation and amortization, was RMB 36 million. Net loss attributable to HUYA Inc. was RMB 118 million, with a non-GAAP net loss of RMB 8 million. Non-GAAP diluted net loss per ADS was RMB 0.04 for Q4.
 	* Cash, short- and long-term deposits stood at RMB 3.82 billion as of December 31, 2025. A 2026 special cash dividend of USD 0.135 per share/ADS was declared, and 22.9 million shares were repurchased for USD 75.5 million under a USD 100 million share buyback program.

Q&A

 	* Rebecca Xu, Morgan Stanley: Asked about profit fluctuations due to provisioning and impairments. CFO Peng responded that "these 2 items are noncash accounting adjustments based on management's highly prudent and critical evaluation... these are more like one-off impacts that do not reflect our core operating trends."
 	* Meng Wei, CICC: Asked about metrics and KPIs for Goose Goose Duck. Huang shared that DAU and retention "exceeding our expectations" with monetization still limited but ARPU spikes during events and updates. Expansion includes a WeChat mini-game version and building a UGC ecosystem.
 	* Ritchie Sun, HSBC: Inquired about AI progress. Huang stated AI-powered channels now "contribute nearly 10% of our overall DAU" and outperform real-life peers by 40% on key metrics.
 	* Fuk Lung Cheung, Citigroup: Asked about game publishing strategy. Huang reiterated commitment to content-driven publishing and said, "we have a number of mini games and mobile games in the pipeline for the year."
 	* Yiwen Zhang, China Renaissance: Asked about gross profit margin trends. Peng explained Q4 gross margin improved due to higher advertising share and in-game item profitability, and expects "continued gross margin improvement."
 	* Yifan Ye, CLSA: Queried in-game item sales margin and sustainability. Peng noted over 200% year-over-year growth, improved profitability, exclusive rights partnerships, and optimization of channel mix.

SENTIMENT ANALYSIS

 	* Analysts pressed on the sustainability of new growth drivers, margin sustainability, and AI progress, with a neutral to slightly positive tone, especially regarding profitability and monetization.
 	* Management's tone was confident in prepared remarks and Q&A, highlighting "strong conviction" in publishing growth, but also cautious and transparent when discussing one-off losses and limited current monetization.
 	* Compared to the previous quarter, analysts' tone was more focused on margin improvement and new growth streams, while management moved from cautious optimism to more assertive confidence in strategic initiatives and technology integration.

QUARTER-OVER-QUARTER COMPARISON

 	* Guidance language shifted to emphasize the strategic role of game publishing and content-driven marketing, compared to the previous quarter’s focus on stabilizing live streaming revenues.
 	* Management’s confidence increased, with repeated references to successful new launches, AI integration, and exclusive partnerships. The previous quarter focused more on ecosystem expansion and initial game publishing steps.
 	* Key metrics showed net revenues grew to RMB 1.74 billion from RMB 1.69 billion, and non-GAAP net income swung to a small loss from a gain, largely due to one-off provisions and lower interest income.
 	* Analysts shifted attention from overall segment growth and platform expansion to more in-depth questions about profitability drivers, the sustainability of new initiatives, and technological advantages.

RISKS AND CONCERNS

 	* CFO Peng cited a "RMB 66 million one-off provision related to a receivable arising from 2021 arrangement with broadcaster" and impairment loss of RMB 81 million as noncash accounting adjustments, not expected to recur.
 	* Management acknowledged the current limited monetization of Goose Goose Duck Mobile and pressure on live streaming gross margin but expects future improvement with business mix shift and upcoming game content updates.
 	* Analysts sought clarity on the sustainability of growth in game publishing and in-game item sales as well as the impact of exclusive partnerships and channel mix changes.

FINAL TAKEAWAY

Management underscored a record year marked by strategic transformation into an integrated game services provider, robust revenue growth, and successful new game launches. The company highlighted a strong pipeline for future game releases, growing influence in AI-powered content, and expanding exclusive partnerships, all while maintaining a stable user base and strengthening its financial position through dividends and share buybacks. The outlook centers on scaling monetization from new titles, driving higher-margin business segments, and leveraging content-driven publishing to propel the next phase of sustainable growth.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/huya/earnings/transcripts]

title: HUYA Q4 Earnings Call Highlights
content:
HUYA logo

Key Points

In Q4 HUYA reported total net revenue of CNY 1.74 billion, up 16% year‑over‑year, driven by a 59% jump in game-related services and advertising to CNY 593 million while live‑streaming remained stable at CNY 1.15 billion. Gross profit improved to CNY 245 million (gross margin 40.1%), but results were hit by a one‑time CNY 66 million provision and CNY 81 million impairments; net loss narrowed with non‑GAAP diluted loss per ADS of CNY 0.04. HUYA is expanding beyond live streaming into publishing and AI—its Goose Goose Duck Mobile title drew >5 million users in 24 hours and >10 million in six days—and returned capital via a $0.135 per ADS special dividend and $75.5 million of repurchases under a $100 million buyback program. Interested in HUYA Inc. Sponsored ADR? Here are five stocks we like better.

HUYA (NYSE:HUYA) executives told investors the company delivered what management called a “record year” in 2025, with fourth-quarter revenue reaching its highest level in 10 quarters as game-related services and advertising continued to expand alongside a stable core live-streaming business.

Fourth-quarter growth led by game-related services and advertising

In the fourth quarter, HUYA reported total net revenues of CNY 1.74 billion, an increase of 16% year over year. Management said live streaming remained stable while the company’s transformation toward a broader gaming-services model accelerated.

→ Data Storage to Data Intelligence: Everpure's Big AI Era Rebrand

Acting co-CEO and CFO Raymond Lei said live streaming revenues rose 2% year over year to CNY 1.15 billion, which he attributed primarily to higher average spending per paying user. Domestic paying users were 4.4 million in the quarter, which the company said excluded certain in-game purchasers through its game distribution business and overseas paying users.

Game-related services, advertising, and other revenues increased 59% year over year to CNY 593 million. Lei said the increase was driven mainly by higher revenue from game-related services and advertising, reflecting deeper cooperation with game companies in China and overseas.

Margins and expenses: provision and impairment weighed on results

→ Dollar Tree Planted the Seeds for Triple-Digit Gains in Q4

Cost of revenues rose 30% year over year to CNY 1.49 billion, which management attributed to higher revenue-sharing fees and content costs as well as increased costs related to in-game items. Revenue-sharing fees and content costs increased 10% year over year to CNY 1.28 billion.

HUYA reported gross profit of CNY 245 million, up 44% year over year. Lei said gross margin improved to 40.1% in the quarter, compared with 11.4% a year earlier. Excluding share-based compensation, non-GAAP gross profit was CNY 248 million and non-GAAP gross margin was 40.3%.

Story Continues

→ The S&P 500's 3 Best-Performing Stocks So Far in 2026

Operating results were pressured by a one-time provision. General and administrative expenses increased 55% year over year to CNY 126 million, primarily due to a CNY 66 million provision related to a receivable stemming from a 2021 arrangement with a broadcaster that management deemed to have heightened non-recoverability risk. In response to an analyst question, management characterized the item as a non-cash accounting adjustment and said it did not reflect core operating trends, adding that it did not see additional large impairments required “at the moment,” while noting it would continue regular reviews under accounting standards.

HUYA also recorded CNY 81 million of impairment losses on investments in the quarter, which Lei said was primarily tied to weak performance at certain U.S. investees.

Other income was CNY 80 million versus CNY 4 million a year earlier, which the company attributed mainly to increased government subsidies. Interest income declined to CNY 32 million from CNY 75 million in the prior-year quarter, which management linked to a lower time deposit balance following special cash dividend payments.

Operating loss narrowed to CNY 65 million from CNY 93 million a year earlier. Excluding share-based compensation and amortization of intangibles from acquisitions, non-GAAP operating loss narrowed to CNY 36 million from CNY 69 million.

On the bottom line, the company reported a net loss attributable to HUYA Inc. that management said narrowed year over year. HUYA also reported diluted net loss per ADS of CNY 0.51 and non-GAAP diluted net loss per ADS of CNY 0.04. As of Dec. 31, 2025, HUYA said it had CNY 3.82 billion in cash and cash equivalents, short-term deposits, and long-term deposits, largely flat versus Sept. 30, 2025.

Full-year 2025: revenue returned to growth

For full-year 2025, HUYA reported total net revenues of CNY 6.5 billion, up 7% from the prior year. Live streaming revenues were CNY 4.59 billion, down from CNY 4.75 billion in the prior year, while game-related services, advertising, and other revenues increased to CNY 1.91 billion from CNY 1.33 billion.

Non-GAAP gross profit was CNY 884 million, up 7%, and non-GAAP gross margin was 30.6%, flat year over year. Non-GAAP net income attributable to HUYA was CNY 99 million, down from CNY 269 million in the prior year. Lei said the decline was largely due to the provision and lower interest income rather than a change in core business performance.

Net cash used in operating activities was CNY 176 million in 2025, compared with net cash provided by operating activities of CNY 94 million in the prior year, which management attributed primarily to lower interest income and higher amounts due from related parties.

Strategy update: publishing, in-game items, esports, and AI

Management emphasized that HUYA has moved beyond being solely a game live-streaming platform, describing its evolution into an “integrated game services provider” with broader reach across platforms. HUYA said it had approximately 160 million monthly active users in the fourth quarter.

Executives highlighted the January launch of Goose Goose Duck Mobile as a milestone in its entry into game publishing. Management said the title attracted more than 5 million new users within the first 24 hours and exceeded 10 million within six days, and that it ranked No. 1 on the iOS free game chart for most of the time since launch. The company credited a content-driven publishing approach, including streamer partnerships and a live-streaming variety show, and said social buzz generated hundreds of millions of impressions and more than 100 trending moments on platforms including Douyin and Red Note. Management said monetization is currently limited as it focuses on engagement, with expectations that monetization will increase after major content updates later this year.

In response to analyst questions, management said the game’s DAU has remained “high and steady,” with retention exceeding expectations, and it anticipates another DAU jump during the summer season tied to events and content updates. The company said it plans to extend the game’s lifecycle through rapid content updates, multi-platform user growth (including a WeChat mini-game version expected to launch later this year), and building a UGC ecosystem via an editor tool.

In in-game item sales, management said revenue grew more than 200% year over year, driven by titles including Peacekeeper Elite and CrossFire Mobile. The company also pointed to exclusive rights and localized partnerships, including becoming the first platform to secure exclusive pre-sale rights for the MVP skin in Honor of Kings. During the Q&A, management said the skin launch generated close to CNY 10 million in gross billings within the first hour and helped drive new paying users. HUYA also said it expects a higher mix of transactions to move into its proprietary in-game item mall over time.

Within esports, HUYA said it offered close to 100 licensed tournaments and around 40 self-produced tournaments and variety shows in the fourth quarter. A key highlight was hosting the Demacia Cup for the first time, which management said marked the first time the League of Legends organizer granted hosting rights to a third-party live-streaming platform. HUYA also cited its Delta Force esports events as setting new bars for the title’s esports circuit.

On product and technology, executives described progress in AI-powered live streaming, including AI hosts with human-like avatars for esports programs. In the Q&A, management said AI-powered channels contribute nearly 10% of overall DAU and that, on average, they outperform comparable channels by about 40% across metrics such as viewing time, retention, and attributed DAU, with top AI channels reaching about 80% outperformance. HUYA also discussed AI game tools such as its Delta Force Map Tool and said it planned to add an AI-powered real-time navigation feature in March. Management also referenced development of AI-enabled smart hardware based on the Goose Goose Duck IP and exploration of multi-agent systems in party game scenarios.

Dividend and buyback

Lei said HUYA’s board declared a 2026 special cash dividend of $0.135 per ordinary share (or $0.135 per ADS), totaling approximately $31 million, under its 2025–2027 dividend plan adopted in March 2025.

Under an up to $100 million share repurchase program, HUYA said it had repurchased 22.9 million shares for an aggregate consideration of $75.5 million as of the end of December 2025.

About HUYA (NYSE:HUYA)

HUYA Inc is a leading interactive live streaming platform based in Guangzhou, China, primarily focused on video game and esports content. The company operates a proprietary technology platform that enables users to broadcast and view live gameplay, participate in real-time chat, and engage with hosts through virtual gifting. Its services are accessible via web browsers, desktop applications and mobile apps for both iOS and Android.

At the core of HUYA's business are user-generated live streams hosted by professional gamers, influencers and esports organizations.

The article "HUYA Q4 Earnings Call Highlights" was originally published by MarketBeat.

title: Earnings week ahead: FDX, BABA, XPEV, MU, GIS, DOCU, OKLO, ACN, and more
content:
[Quarterly Report Financial Analysis and Business Performance Review with Office Tools]
Mohamad Faizal Bin Ramli/iStock via Getty Images

Wall Street has entered a quieter phase of the earnings calendar, but the macro environment is shouting. While the earnings calendar has thinned out, the upcoming week still brings key reports from a diverse set of companies across various sectors: semiconductors, cloud services, retail, logistics, and clean energy, offering investors fresh insights into global demand trends and corporate spending.

Chipmaker Micron Technology (MU [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MU]) will be closely watched for signals on memory demand and the AI-driven data center cycle.

In Asia, results from Alibaba Group Holding Limited (BABA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BABA]) and Tencent Holdings Limited (TCEHY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/TCEHY]) could provide a read on China’s internet and e-commerce landscape, while EV maker XPeng (XPEV [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/XPEV]) will offer insight into competition within China’s fast-growing electric vehicle market.

Enterprise and professional services companies will also be in focus, including Accenture (ACN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ACN]) and Docusign (DOCU [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/DOCU]), which may shed light on corporate technology spending and digital transformation trends.

On the consumer side, retailers and brands such as lululemon athletica (LULU [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/LULU]), Macy's (M [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/M]), and Dollar Tree (DLTR [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/DLTR]) are expected to provide clues on discretionary spending and value-oriented shopping behavior, while packaged food giant General Mills (GIS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/GIS]) will offer perspective on staples demand and pricing trends.

Logistics and transportation demand will be highlighted by results from FedEx (FDX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/FDX]), often viewed as a barometer for global trade and economic activity.

Meanwhile, clean energy and emerging technology names, including Canadian Solar (CSIQ [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CSIQ]), Oklo (OKLO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/OKLO]), Red Cat Holdings (RCAT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/RCAT]), and satellite imaging company Planet Labs PBC (PL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/PL]), will round out a diverse reporting calendar.

With companies spanning technology, consumer goods, logistics, and energy, the week’s results could provide investors with a broad snapshot of economic momentum and sector-specific trends heading into the next quarter.

Below is a rundown of major quarterly updates anticipated in the week of March 16 to March 20:

MONDAY, MARCH 16

Dollar Tree (DLTR [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/DLTR]) is scheduled to report FQ4 results before the market opens on Monday, with analysts expecting profits to rise more than 10% despite a roughly 34% decline in revenue.

Wall Street analysts maintain a Hold rating on the stock. Meanwhile, Seeking Alpha’s Quant Rating recently downgraded it to Buy [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/DLTR/ratings/quant-ratings#source=firstLevelUrl%3Asymbol%7CbiSectionAsset%3ARatingSummary%7CbiName%3AQuant_rating] from Strong Buy ahead of earnings, citing valuation concerns.

Seeking Alpha author Caffital Research rates the stock Hold [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4880852-dollar-tree-lets-see-how-the-strategy-performs], noting that Dollar Tree is likely to post solid Q4 earnings supported by strong sales momentum and holiday-season assortments. The key focus will be the company’s FY2026 guidance, as strategic initiatives, tariff changes, and a volatile consumer environment create a somewhat uncertain but generally positive outlook. Caffital Research estimates fair value for DLTR at about $134.80 per share.

 	* Consensus EPS Estimates: $2.53
 	* Consensus Revenue Estimates: $5.47B
 	* Earnings Insight: The company has exceeded EPS in 6 of the past 8 quarters and revenue in 5 of those reports.

TUESDAY, MARCH 17

DOCUSIGN (DOCU [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/DOCU])

DocuSign (DOCU [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/DOCU]) is set to report Q4 earnings after the market closes on Tuesday, with analysts expecting year-over-year growth in both EPS and revenue.

Recently, Needham & Company said DocuSign is testing a new pricing [https://seekingalpha.com/news/4562787-docusign-ponders-new-pricing-for-esignature-pro-tier-needham] and packaging model for its eSignature Professional tier. According to analyst Scott Berg, the company is experimenting with a potential 50% price increase while raising the annual envelope limit from 100 to unlimited. Management confirmed pricing tests are underway but noted that no final decisions have been made. Needham said the impact is difficult to quantify due to limited data, though the initiative could support growth in FY2027 and FY2028. The testing does not involve Docusign’s Intelligent Agreement Management (IAM) platform, its AI-powered system for workflow automation, document analysis, and agreement data integration.

Both Wall Street analysts and Seeking Alpha’s Quant Rating maintain a Hold rating [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/DOCU/ratings/quant-ratings#source=firstLevelUrl%3Asymbol%7CbiSectionAsset%3ARatingSummary%7CbiName%3AQuant_rating], reflecting caution around competition and valuation.

However, Seeking Alpha contributor Brett Ashcroft-Green maintains a Buy [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4880412-docusign-earnings-preview-for-an-anti-bubble-poster-child], arguing that DocuSign's valuation has compressed significantly despite steady revenue growth and improving free cash flow. He also notes that AI disruption concerns may be overstated, as digital agreement infrastructure remains deeply embedded in enterprise software ecosystems.

Ashcroft-Green adds that expanding international adoption and growth in the IAM platform could provide new revenue streams beyond core e-signature services. With a strong balance sheet, net cash position, and high gross margins, DocuSign also has financial flexibility during a slower SaaS growth cycle.

 	* Consensus EPS Estimates: $0.95
 	* Consensus Revenue Estimates: $828.22M
 	* Earnings Insight: The company has consistently outperformed EPS and revenue expectations in the past 8 quarters.

WEDNESDAY, MARCH 18

MICRON TECHNOLOGY (MU [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MU])

Micron Technology (MU [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MU]) is set to release FQ2 results after the closing bell on Wednesday, with analysts expecting profits to surge 452% Y/Y on 138% revenue growth, driven by strong AI-related demand for memory chips, particularly DRAM and high-bandwidth memory (HBM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HBM]).

Ahead of the report, Citigroup reiterated its Buy [https://seekingalpha.com/news/4562317-micron-sees-bullish-views-at-citi-ahead-of-earnings] rating and raised its price target to $430 from $385, citing rising memory prices and robust AI demand. Susquehanna International Group also lifted its target to $525 from $345 while maintaining a positive rating.

Meanwhile, GF Securities expects the memory cycle to remain “robust, [https://seekingalpha.com/news/4562649-micron-likely-to-reiterate-robust-memory-cycle-for-q2-and-beyond-gf-securities]” forecasting DRAM contract prices to jump about 100% in Q1 2026, followed by more than 30% sequential growth in Q2. Analyst Jeff Pu raised his price target to $571, projecting $23B in Q2 revenue with 77% gross margins, and sees Q3 revenue potentially reaching $29B with margins expanding further. Pu also noted Micron has likely secured early HBM4 orders.

Seeking Alpha’s Quant Rating maintains a Strong Buy [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MU/ratings/quant-ratings#source=firstLevelUrl%3Asymbol%7CbiSectionAsset%3ARatingSummary%7CbiName%3AQuant_rating], broadly aligned with bullish sell-side sentiment.

Seeking Alpha contributor Pythia Research also rates the stock a Strong Buy, pointing to strong fiscal Q1 [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4881453-microns-next-chapter-starts-after-q2] results with $13.6B revenue, 56.8% gross margin, $4.78 EPS, and a record $3.9B in free cash flow. AI-driven demand has tightened supply, with HBM capacity sold out through 2026, while high-value segments such as cloud and data-center memory continue to expand rapidly.

However, Seeking Alpha contributor Deep Value Investing downgraded the stock to Hold [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4879327-micron-technology-stock-samsung-hbm4-memory-chip-downside-catalyst], warning that Samsung Electronics’s HBM4 production ramp could pressure Micron’s market share. After a 215% rally since last summer, the analyst argues Micron may need consistent beat-and-raise quarters to sustain momentum.

 	* Consensus EPS Estimates: $8.61
 	* Consensus Revenue Estimates: $19.13B
 	* Earnings Insight: Micron has beaten EPS and revenue expectations in 8 consecutive quarters.

THURSDAY, MARCH 19

ALIBABA GROUP HOLDING (BABA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BABA])

Alibaba Group Holding Limited (BABA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BABA]) is set to report FQ3 results on Thursday before the market opens, offering a key read on China’s consumer environment and sentiment toward Chinese technology stocks. Analysts expect revenue to rise about 9% Y/Y, while profits are projected to decline roughly 45%, pointing to a mixed near-term outlook.

The company recently launched a dedicated mobile app enabling users to deploy OpenClaw within minutes, highlighting intensifying competition among Chinese tech giants in the emerging agentic AI assistant space.

Morgan Stanley recently named [https://seekingalpha.com/news/4563734-alibaba-named-as-top-pick-at-morgan-stanley-as-firm-calls-it-global-ai-winner] Alibaba its top pick among Chinese tech companies, maintaining an Overweight rating and calling it a potential “global AI winner.”

Wall Street analysts broadly rate the stock as a Strong Buy, while Seeking Alpha’s Quant Rating is more cautious at Hold [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BABA/ratings/quant-ratings#source=firstLevelUrl%3Asymbol%7CbiSectionAsset%3ARatingSummary%7CbiName%3AQuant_rating].

Seeking Alpha contributor Oakoff Investments maintains a Buy [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4880402-alibabas-q3-earnings-could-trigger-a-rebound-preview], expecting Alibaba to beat conservative Q3 estimates and provide upbeat commentary, supported by cloud growth, improving quick-commerce margins, and strong user retention. The analyst sets a $153.51 price target, implying about 15–16% upside.

However, another contributor, Geneva Investor, rates the stock a Sell [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4880368-alibaba-macro-perspective-on-an-uninvestable-stock], citing Alibaba’s heavy reliance on China’s domestic economy and ongoing macro and geopolitical uncertainties that could weigh on long-term growth.

 	* Consensus EPS Estimates: $1.59
 	* Consensus Revenue Estimates: $42.20B
 	* Earnings Insight: The company has beaten EPS and revenue estimates in 3 of the past 8 quarters.

FRIDAY, MARCH 20

XPENG (XPEV [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/XPEV])

XPeng (XPEV [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/XPEV]) is set to report FQ4 results before the market opens on Friday, with analysts expecting profits to improve by about 96% Y/Y on more than 43% revenue growth.

Recently, Morgan Stanley shared positive remarks [https://seekingalpha.com/news/4562356-xpeng-rallies-after-showing-off-its-new-next-gen-autonomous-driving-model] after testing the company’s VLA 2.0 autonomous driving system in Guangzhou. The next-generation Vision-Language-Action AI model is designed as an end-to-end vision-to-action stack aimed at enabling Level 4-grade autonomous driving and broader embodied AI applications. The technology is expected to roll out on XPeng’s P7, G7, and X9 Ultra models in the second half of the year.

Earlier this month, XPeng reported [https://seekingalpha.com/news/4559074-xpeng-february-deliveries-slide-499-yy-to-15256-units-following-lunar-new-year-global-p7-rollout-begins] February deliveries of 15,256 vehicles, down 49.9% Y/Y and about 23% lower than January’s 20,011 units, highlighting ongoing volatility in monthly sales trends.

Just days ahead of earnings, Seeking Alpha’s Quant Rating upgraded the stock to Buy [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/XPEV/ratings/quant-ratings#source=firstLevelUrl%3Asymbol%7CbiSectionAsset%3ARatingSummary%7CbiName%3AQuant_rating] from Hold, aligning with bullish sell-side sentiment.

Seeking Alpha contributor Michael Ngan also maintains a Buy [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4862203-xpeng-not-just-a-car-company], noting that XPeng’s performance since the second half of 2024 supports its competitive positioning. While domestic EV sales growth may stabilize, the analyst sees additional upside from emerging businesses such as AeroHT, robotaxis, and humanoid robotics, which could add about $7B in enterprise value. Ngan sets a price target of $25.50 for XPEV.

 	* Consensus EPS Estimates: -$0.01
 	* Consensus Revenue Estimates: $3.19B
 	* Earnings Insight: The company has beaten EPS estimates in 7 of the past 8 quarters and revenue estimates in 4 of those reports.
